title: Cannot set up namespace using /usr/bin/newuidmap - Rocky 9.2 Kernel 5.14 Podman 4.4.1
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

When running any podman command (aiming for rootless podman), we get:
`ERRO[0000] running /usr/bin/newuidmap <pid> 0 83060 1 1 608752 65536`
`Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1`

I've been getting this issue for a couple of weeks now, since updating my server to Rocky 9.2.
We have another server that is configured by someone else that is also running Rocky 9.2, this one seems to be working. But the error we are seeing is reproducible on a rebuild of the server.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Execute any podman command. Including podman system migrate


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

Expected podman commands to run, e.g podman ps, would list running containers

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

We cannot run podman info as a user, but as root, we get:
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

Kernel 5.14
Podman 4.4.1

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
/etc/subuid and /etc/subgid look like:
`user1:150000:65536`
`user2:215536:65536`
etc etc

And an `ls -lahZ` on the files shows:
`-rw-r--r-- 1 root root ? 166 Jul 24 14:20 /etc/subgid`
`-rw-r--r-- 1 root root ? 166 Jul 24 14:20 /etc/subuid`

```
getfattr -d -m- /usr/bin/newuidmap 
getfattr: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names
# file: usr/bin/newuidmap
security.capability=0sAQAAAoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=
security.selinux="system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0"
```

```
stat /usr/bin/newuidmap 
  File: /usr/bin/newuidmap
  Size: 38848     	Blocks: 88         IO Block: 4096   regular file
Device: fd00h/64768d	Inode: 403742    Links: 1
Access: (0755/-rwxr-xr-x)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2023-07-24 02:02:02.235963776 +0100
Modify: 2023-04-16 05:17:10.000000000 +0100
Change: 2023-07-21 14:16:47.180503719 +0100
 Birth: 2023-07-17 12:07:47.179503644 +0100
```

```
grep /run /proc/self/mountinfo

28 65 0:25 / /run rw,nosuid,nodev shared:10 - tmpfs tmpfs rw, size=78861500k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
```

```
$ unshare -U sleep 100 &
$ newuidmap $! 0 608572 65536
$ newgidmap $! 0 608572 65536

```
The above statements all work fine

Log Level Debug:
```
podman --log-level debug ps

INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --root /data/podman-store/user1 --log-level debug ps)
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /data/podman-store/user1/libpod/bolt_state.db
DEBU[0000] systemd-logind: Unknown object '/'.
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver vfs
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /data/podman-store/user1
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/run-83715
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /data/podman-store/user1/libpod
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/83715/libpod/tmp
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /data/podman-store/user1/volumes
DEBU[0000] Using transient path: false
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend file
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun-wasm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-wasm: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime youki initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime youki: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"
DEBU[0000] systemd-logind: Unknown object '/'.
DEBU[0000] Invalid systemd user session for current user
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 5602 0 83715 1 1 281072 65536`
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

I see you have used a different range when trying it out manually:

```
 $ unshare -U sleep 100 &
$ newuidmap $! 0 608572 65536
$ newgidmap $! 0 608572 65536
```

compared to what is used by Podman:

```
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 5602 0 83715 1 1 281072 65536`
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
```

What do you get if you try the same values with the same user?

```
 $ unshare -U sleep 100 &
$ newuidmap $! 0 83715 1 1 281072 65536
```

How was the session created?  Is the `USER` environment variable correct?

The USER environment variable is correct. And I get the same output (successful) result for executing the correct neuidmap command (I had swapped users when attempting it before to see if I got the same results across multiple users)

don't see why it is any different than podman executing it.

Is there any useful information in the journal?

Could you try the latest upstream version?

Neither do I, its quite the conundrum.

Journalctl returns:
```
systemd[1]: Starting Podman API Service...
systemd[1]: Started Podman API Service.
podman[82852]: level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
podman[82852]: level=info msg="Not using native diff for overlay, this may cause degraded performance for building images: kernel has CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_REDIRECT_DIR enabled"
podman[82852]: level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 97"
podman[82852]: level=info msg="API service listening on \"/run/podman/podman.sock\". URI: \"/run/podman/podman.sock\""
podman[82852]: "GET /info HTTP/1.1" 200 2382 "" ""
systemd[1]: podman.service: Deactivated successfully.
```

This repeats itself every 10 mins

We are using the latest packaged version for R9.2

I am just looking to run the latest version now, but need to wait for it to import to our systems
Installed latest podman 4.6.0-rc2, and still getting the same errors

Upon further investigation into debugging, I should iterate that the server we are running this on has home directories NFS mounted.

We have a libpod.conf file in ~/.config/containers/ set as:
```
volume_path = "/data/podman-store/<user-id>/volumes"
image_default_transport = "docker://"
runtime = "runc"
conmon_path = ["/usr/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon", "/usr/bin/conmon", "/usr/sbin/conmon", "/usr/local/bin/conmon", "/usr/local/sbin/conmon"]
conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
init_path = "/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit"
static_dir = "/data/podman-store/<user-id>/libpod"
tmp_dir = "/tmp/run-<uid>/libpod/tmp"
max_log_size = -1
no_pivot_root = false
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
cni_plugin_dir = ["/usr/libexec/cni", "/usr/lib/cni", "/usr/local/lib/cni", "/opt/cni/bin"]
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
infra_command = "/pause"
enable_port_reservation = true
label = true
network_cmd_path = ""
num_locks = 2048
events_logger = "journald"
EventsLogFilePath = ""
detach_keys = "ctrl-p,ctrl-q"

[runtime]
  runc = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc"]
```

We also have a storage.conf located in the same space:

```
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  runroot = "/run/user/<uid>
  graphroot = "/data/podman-store/<user-id>/storage"
  rootless_storage_path = "/data/podman-store/<user-id>/storage"
  [storage.options]
    [storage.options.overlay]
      mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

Hopefully this information can assist in further debugging

I have tried this on a Rocky 9.2 build with no nfs mounted home directory, and this still breaks.
After attempting this on a Rocky 8.7 build running podman 4.2.0, everything works, both with and without a home directory.

I am still not able to reproduce it locally.

Since you are able to reproduce it so easily, could you try to bisect v4.2 and v.4.4.0 and see what commit is causing the issue for you?

Apologies to burst the bubble, but it seems that upon running Rocky 9.1 (where podman 4.2.0 is available) we still see the same issue. So it seems it may not be podman itself, but rather podmans compatibility with Rocky 9.X. At least with our config outlined in the previous comments
would it be possible for you to try reproducing it with CentOS Stream and the same configuration?

Hello,

Thanks for all the assistance, we have managed to find the issue/solution. It seems some interaction of a podman dependancy or podman itself between RHEL 7/8 and RHEL 9 requires that /etc/login.defs be world readable (0644) which is the standard behaviour of that file, but some security polices, ensure that the file is just readable to user and group (0640).
Changing this to 0644 fixes the issues we were seeing.

Hopefully this can help either insert a better debug, or find a work around, or just leave it as is.

Otherwise, the ticket can be closed. And thanks again.
I have a feeling this is something to do with newuidmap.
thanks for letting us know.  I am closing the issue now
